title: The Nobel Peace Prize Was Awarded to the World Food Programme
subtitle: 和平奖为何颁给了一个「喂饭」的机构

paragraphs.en: In 2020, the Nobel Peace Prize was awarded to the World Food Programme (WFP). Why a Nobel Prize for the WFP, and why now? In 2019, the WFP assisted nearly 100 million people in 88 countries. It is the safety net for those who fall off the edge of existence. It is a response to solving the problem of food instability.
paragraphs.zh: 2020年，诺贝尔和平奖授予了世界粮食计划署。为什么是粮食计划署？为什么是现在？2019年，粮食计划署向88个国家的近1亿人提供了援助。它是那些处于生存边缘的人们的安全网，也是应对粮食不稳定问题的重要举措。

paragraphs.en: Its Nobel Prize reminds us all of the moral hazard in imagining that the poor and vulnerable are somebody else's problem. The WFP has been around since 1961 and has been the global coordinator of nationally based efforts to avoid disasters with food aid. Despite decades of effort to eliminate hunger, the latest estimate is that about 11% of people on the planet (about 820 million people) are suffering daily undernourishment.
paragraphs.zh: 这项诺贝尔奖提醒我们，将穷人和弱势群体视为别人的问题是一种道德风险。粮食计划署自1961年成立以来，一直是各国通过粮食援助避免灾难的全球协调者。尽管人们为消除饥饿已努力了数十年，但最新估计显示，全球约11%的人口（约8.2亿人）仍在每天忍受营养不良。

paragraphs.en: Progress at reducing undernourishment has stopped despite gains through the 1990s and 2000s. Developed countries sometimes offer food and aid to developing ones, but at a price. One American philosopher stated that addressing the needs of the poor and vulnerable is about more than money — it is mostly about creating conditions under which prosperity and opportunity can thrive.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管20世纪90年代和2000年代曾取得进展，但减少营养不良的努力已经停滞。发达国家有时会向发展中国家提供粮食和援助，但往往附带条件。一位美国哲学家指出，解决穷人和弱势群体的需求不仅仅是金钱问题，更关键的是创造让繁荣和机会茁壮成长的条件。

words.meaning: v. 蓬勃发展；茁壮成长

paragraphs.en: When aid is offered with heavy conditions attached, like loan repayment or food for resources, it often widens the gap between rich and poor and sustains the old world order. This is why the work of the WFP is so vital. The scientific community, however, can provide a helping hand to the WFP.
paragraphs.zh: 当援助附带苛刻条件时，比如贷款偿还或以粮食换取资源，往往会拉大贫富差距并维护旧的世界秩序。这就是粮食计划署的工作如此重要的原因。然而，科学界可以为粮食计划署提供助力。

paragraphs.en: By sharing knowledge of agriculture and climate with peers in countries most vulnerable to poverty and hunger, scientists can help reduce these problems. By making its voice heard, science can lead by example. The ability to overcome food shortages that must be built into some of the poorest countries will not come from loans from wealthy countries, which may have food problems of their own, or world economic institutions. This ability will be built upon self-confident people using open and shared scientific knowledge to pull themselves out of their misery.
paragraphs.zh: 通过与最易受贫困和饥饿影响的国家的同行分享农业和气候知识，科学家可以帮助缓解这些问题。科学通过发出自己的声音，可以起到表率作用。最贫困国家必须建立克服粮食短缺的能力，但这种能力不会来自富裕国家或世界经济机构的贷款——因为富裕国家自身也可能面临粮食问题。这种能力将建立在自信的人们利用开放共享的科学知识摆脱困境的基础上。

words.meaning: adj. 易受伤害的；脆弱的
